title: Anthropic Co-Founder Reveals Briefings to Trump Admin on Mythos Amid Lawsuit
summary: Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark confirmed the company briefed the Trump administration on its AI model Mythos, despite ongoing legal action against the U.S. government. The revelation highlights the complex relationship between AI firms and federal regulators.
body:
In an interview at the Semafor World Economy summit this week, Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark disclosed that the company had briefed the Trump administration on its advanced AI model, Mythos. This admission comes as Anthropic continues to sue the U.S. government over regulatory policies, creating a paradoxical situation where the company engages with the very entity it is legally challenging.

The disclosure underscores the intricate dynamics between AI developers and government oversight. While Anthropic seeks to influence policy through direct engagement, its lawsuit suggests a deep-seated disagreement with current regulatory frameworks. This dual approach may set a precedent for how other AI firms navigate the tension between collaboration and litigation with federal agencies.

Moving forward, the implications of this revelation could shape the future of AI governance. Industry observers will be watching to see if other companies follow Anthropic's lead, balancing direct briefings with legal challenges. The outcome of the lawsuit and the nature of future briefings will likely determine the trajectory of AI regulation in the coming years.
